---
title: Project Review Template 
author: Talia C. Swanson
date: date-modified
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
---

# Overview

Title of project: Asssessing the Role of Age and Cumulative Smoking Exposure in Lung Cancer Risk.

Name of project author(s): Saara Al Jawad

Name of project reviewer: Talia C. Swanson


# Specific project content evaluation
Evaluate the different parts of the project by filling in the sections below.


## Background, Context and Motivation
How well is the context of the project described? Is a comprehensive background, including summary of previous/related work given? Is the project well placed into the context of existing work (including proper referencing of existing work). Is it clear why the project was undertaken and what new information it hopes to provide?

### Feedback and Comments

The project provides a strong and well-developed background on lung cancer risk factors, with clear emphasis on age, smoking exposure, and COPD. The introduction is well supported by relevant literature highlights the need for improved risk measurement using pack-years. The research context is clearly established. Overall, the motivation is well explained and appropriate.

### Summary assessment (PICK ONE, DELETE THE OTHERS)

* strong contextualization and motivation


## Question description
How well and clear are the question(s)/hypotheses the project aims to address described? Is it clear how the questions relate to the data?


### Feedback and Comments

The research question is clearly stated and well aligned with the data and analysis approach. It appropriately focuses on the comparative predictive value of age, smoking status, and cumulative smoking exposure. The hypothesis is explicitly stated. Overall, well done.

### Summary assessment

* question/hypotheses fully clear


## Data description
How well is the data overall described?  Is the source provided? Is a codebook or other meta-information available that makes it clear what the data is? 

### Feedback and Comments

The dataset is clearly described, including sample size, variables, and key features relevant to the analysis. The author identifies primary exposures and outcomes and explains the inclusion of COPD.

### Summary assessment

* source and overall structure of data well explained


## Data wrangling and exploratory analysis
How well is the data cleaned/processed and explored? Are all steps reasonable and well explained? Are alternatives discussed and considered? Are meaningful exploratory results shown (e.g. in the supplementary materials)?

### Feedback and Comments

I recommend including a section in your README file on how to run things, just so that it is easier for the user. The data cleaning and prep are clearly described. Overall, well done. 

### Summary assessment

* essentially no weaknesses in wrangling and exploratory component



## Appropriateness of Analysis
Were the analysis methods appropriate for the data? Was the analysis done properly? Were different components of the analysis (e.g. performance measure, variable selection, data pre-processing, model evaluation) done in the best way possible and explained well?

### Feedback and Comments

The statistical and predictive modeling approaches are appropriate for the research question. Logistic regression is used to assess associations, and the comparison of multiple model is done well. The inclusion of pack-years and COPD is well justified.

### Summary assessment

* strong and reasonable analysis

## Presentation
How well are results presented? Are tables and figures easy to read and understand? Are the main figures/tables publication level quality? 

### Feedback and Comments

Results are very well presented with clear tables, figures, and narrative explanations. Visualizations are informative and effectively support the findings. The interpretation of results is easy to follow. 

### Summary assessment

* results are very well presented


## Discussion/Conclusions
Are the study findings properly discussed? Are strengths and limitations acknowledged? Are findings interpreted properly?

### Feedback and Comments

The discussion is strong, well structured, and clearly summarizes key findings. The interpretation of pack-years as the dominant predictor is well supported by the results. Strengths and limitations are appropriately mentioned, and conclusions are consistent with the analysis. The discussion could be slightly expanded by connecting findings more explicitly to broader public health implications.

### Summary assessment

* strong, complete and clear discussion


## Further comments


# Overall project content evaluation
Evaluate overall features of the project  by filling in the sections below.


## Structure
Is the project well structured? Are files in well labeled folders? Do files have reasonable names? Are all "junk" files not needed for analysis/reproduction removed? By just looking at files and folders, can you get an idea of how things fit together?

### Feedback and Comments

The project is well organized with a clear logical flow from data import to final results. Code, figures, and tables are properly separated and consistently referenced. File structure appears clean and easy to navigate, supporting reproducibility and readability.

### Summary assessment

* well structured


## Documentation 
How well is the project documented? Are you able to understand each step of the whole analysis, each decision that was made, and each line of code? Is enough information provided as comments in code or as part of Rmd files? 

### Feedback and Comments

The project is well documented, with clear explanations provided throughout the manuscript. Each step is described making the workflow easy to understand. 

### Summary assessment

* fully and well documented


## Reproducibility
Are all results fully reproducible? Is documentation provided which clearly explains how to reproduce things, and does it work without the need for any manual intervention? Are you able to re-run the whole analysis without having to do manual interventions/edits?

### Feedback and Comments

The project is highly reproducible, with a clear and structured workflow using paths and organized outputs. The use of train test splits and saved result objects is well documented. The analysis can be reliably reproduced with understanding/little intervention. 


### Summary assessment

* fully reproducible without issues


## Thoroughness
How thorough was the overall study? Were alternatives (e.g. different ways of processing the data or different models) considered? Were alternatives discussed? Were the questions/hypotheses fully and thoroughly addressed?

### Feedback and Comments

The analysis is very thorough with exploratory analysis, multiple logistic regression models, predictive modeling, cross-validation, and model comparison. Overall, the study is comprehensive and well developed.

### Summary assessment

* strong level of thorougness


## Further comments
 Overall great work!
